blocks.text: DTC P0971: Clutch pressure control SOL.V. failure

The clutch pressure control SOL.V. controls the hydraulic pressure in the hydraulic circuit. The spool in the clutch pressure control SOL.V. pushes a valve in response to the duty (load) output, which is converted into a current value by the PGM-FI ECU. As a result, the valve pushed by the spool releases oil pressure proportional to the current value. the PGM-FI ECU measures the current flowing in the linear solenoid of the clutch pressure control SOL.V. and compares it with the commanded current of the duty output and feeds back the resulting difference. the PGM-FI The ECU outputs a clutch pressure control SOL.V. control signal to provide the appropriate forward/reverse clutch hydraulic pressure supply. Failure is caused by a short circuit or temporary short circuit. When the drive circuit of the clutch pressure control SOL.V. is turned on, if the current detection circuit detects an overcurrent in the drive circuit for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a fault and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When the drive circuit of the clutch pressure control SOL.V. is turned on and the current detection circuit detects an overcurrent in the drive circuit for 1 second or longer.

items.text: Short circuit in code (CPCLS line) between PGM-FI ECU and clutch pressure control SOL.V.
Clutch Pressure Control SOL.V. Internal circuit short circuit

blocks.text: Start the engine and wait at least 1 second at standstill.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
